channel.title: Home on Zig SHOWTIME
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Zig SHOWTIME

item.title: dt
item.description: dt is a utility and functional-concatenative programming language. It provides some new ways of scripting and gluing programs together in Unix-like systems like Mac OSX, Linux, and BSD. https://github.com/so-dang-cool/dt

item.title: Pixi
item.description: A nice GUI program, written in Zig, that helps you create art, what’s not to love? https://github.com/foxnne/pixi

item.title: Buzz
item.description: Buzz is a lightweight language written in Zig that compiles to bytecode and runs in a JITted VM. You can learn more on the official website: https://buzz-lang.dev/

item.title: Ghostty
item.description: Mitchell probably doesn’t need any introduction, but when it comes specifically to the Zig community he also has has plenty of interesting content he’s published on his personal blog: Posts on Zig Internals While Ghostty, the subject of his talk, is not openly available yet, he also wrote a few devlogs about it: Ghostty Devlogs
ONE LAST STEP Please confirm your email address! You should have received an email with the activation link. Quick, click it before somebody set us up the bomb!
UNSUBSCRIBED Yeah, emails are annoying. See you around!
YOU ARE NOW SUBSCRIBED For great justice! YOUTUBE The YouTube channel hosts more Zig content and also talks from Software You Can Love

What is Zig SHOWTIME? Zig SHOWTIME is a live-streamed show that airs on Twitch where (mostly) members of the Zig community present on a topic and take questions from the viewers. I've never spoken publicly before. Don't worry, we accept first-time speakers and if you point out that you might need help, we'll be happy to support you. As of now we've aired 3 episodes and for a few of the speakers that was the first time they spoke publicly.

item.title: Social Media
item.description: SUBSCRIBE TO THE NEWSLETTER Get notified when a new episode airs! RSS is also available! READ PAST ISSUES Might include other lightly editorialized content and traces of nuts. Why does the button say SLAP instead of SUBMIT?
